coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Build Phase, Week 1 of 8 — Wednesday calls for a mid-week quality effort. Target 75–90 min with 3×8-min hill repeats at perceived effort 7/10 (think: hard but controlled conversation possible). Walk the descent — Julian Alps will punish quads that haven't practiced downhill loading. If you're in a flat city, find stairs or a treadmill on max incline. Keep the last 20 min at easy Zone 2 to flush legs. Total vert goal: 400–600m. Log your HR drift — you're building the aerobic engine that will carry you through 2,500m of Slovenian alpine gain in September.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Mid-build means your gut needs training too. On today's run, practice taking 30–40g of carbs per hour using whatever you plan to race with — gels, chews, or real food. The Julian Alps course will have limited aid, so self-sufficiency is key. Post-run within 30 minutes: 20–25g protein + fast carbs to kickstart glycogen replenishment. As a full-time traveler, lean on hotel eggs + fruit or Greek yogurt + banana if you're not cooking. Hydration note: if you're crossing time zones right now, electrolyte intake matters more than plain water — sodium especially.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: You're 18 weeks out — deep in the build window where cumulative fatigue is the hidden enemy. Tonight, protect your sleep like it's training. Aim for 8 hours; if travel or meetings compressed yesterday, a 20-min nap before 3pm is clinically proven to restore cognitive function and reduce injury risk. Do 10 minutes of hip flexor + calf work tonight — the alpine terrain in Kranjska Gora will demand full ankle and hip range of motion on technical descents. Foam roll your IT bands if you did the hill repeats. Body check: any niggles that weren't there Monday need to be tracked, not ignored.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: 128 days to race day. Build phase is where races are won — not by grinding recklessly, but by showing up consistently across travel, meetings, and time zones. You don't need a perfect week. You need a good-enough week, repeated 18 times. Today's mental practice: spend 5 minutes before your run visualizing the Julian Alps ridgeline at kilometer 35 — legs heavy, views stunning, summit in sight. That image is your anchor when it gets hard. The athlete who races Julian Alps well is the one who trained their mind to embrace discomfort as signal, not warning. Today is a rep toward that.
